purpose: Machine-readable policy for the WellFit Agent Architect & Proposal Agent. The agent reads existing governance sources, selects exactly one next approved future agent for human review, and generates a safe build-prompt draft without creating agents or modifying runtime product code.

selectionRules.rule: Select only entries from approved-agent-build-backlog.json with status approved_for_build or approved_for_planning.
Sort candidates by suggestedBuildOrder ascending, then priority, then id.
Skip backlog entries with status built, blocked, rejected, duplicate, or superseded.
Read dependencies and protected boundaries as proposal evidence; do not satisfy dependencies by creating files for the selected future agent.
Select and report exactly one next agent at a time; if none are eligible, report NEXT_AGENT_BUILD_PROPOSAL_READY=false.

proposalRules.rule: Proposal entries must map to a source approved backlog id and preserve reason, expected benefit, risk level, allowed files, forbidden files, required docs, required registers, required validation scripts, and human-review boundaries.
Every generated proposal must set humanReviewRequired to true even when a source backlog entry is already approved for planning or build sequencing.
A proposal may describe a future build task but must not create the future agent, mark it built, approve a PR, merge, repair, or deploy.

promptGenerationRules.rule: Generated prompts must include first-read files, allowed files, forbidden files, report-only first-version boundaries, required checks, human review, and explicit no-auto-build/no-auto-merge/no-auto-repair/no-deploy instructions.
Generated prompts must scope implementation to exactly one selected agent and forbid building additional future agents in the same task.
Write the generated prompt to scripts/wellfit-dev-agent/output/next-agent-build-prompt.md and the proposal report to scripts/wellfit-dev-agent/output/next-agent-build-proposal.md.

connectedAgentCountingRules: Count unique connectedAgents from the selected approved backlog entry.
If a connected agent id is also present in agent-catalog.json, preserve that id in the generated report.
Do not infer new connected agents by creating parallel architecture; use existing catalog/backlog references only.
connectedRegisterCountingRules: Count unique connectedRegisters from the selected approved backlog entry.
Include requiredRegisters separately in reports, but connectedRegisterCount must match the connectedRegisters array length.
Do not count generated output markdown files as connected registers.
riskClassificationRules: Start with the riskLevel on the selected approved backlog entry.
Escalate proposal language to human-review-required when the selected entry is medium, high, critical, or protected-boundary related.
Consult risk-classifier.json and product-readiness.json for protected-area language, but do not modify protected logic.
humanReviewRequiredFor: every generated build prompt before it becomes a Codex implementation task

any high, critical, protected-boundary, runtime, compliance, privacy, child, health, location, reward, mission-authority, token, NFT, wallet, payment, betting, Unity, auto-merge, auto-repair, approval, merge, close, or deploy scope
marking a future backlog entry built after implementation evidence is available
